user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for VanArsdel Ltd.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 11:03 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at VanArsdel Ltd

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 14:37 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-02-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:05:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:09:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:23:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:28:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

signals.detail: Signal detected for VanArsdel Ltd: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-Q trap (Daily-Automation generating pre-packs for every meeting creates noise vs filteri); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-Q failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-Q failure mode (Daily-Automation generating pre-packs for every meeting creates noise vs filteri)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
